zh.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett是John le Brett I的女兒，於大約1298年出生在英國英格蘭艾可。她於1320年與William de Wasteneys結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1332年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾可爾頓享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett是John le Brett I的女儿，于大约1298年出生在英国英格兰艾可。她于1320年与William de Wasteneys结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1332年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾可爾頓享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Brett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for a Celtic-speaking Briton or a Breton, from Middle English brit, bret, Old French bret (see Brett ).
German: from a vernacular form of the personal name Brixius (see Brice ).

variants.name: Britting
meanings: American shortened form of South German Brittinger, a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah

In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

en.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett was born about 1298, in Essex, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of John le Brett I. She married William de Wasteneys in 1320. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1332, in Colton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett est née environ 1298, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de John le Brett I. Elle a épousé William de Wasteneys en 1320. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1332, à Colton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett wurde um 1298 in Essex, England, United Kingdom, als Tochter von John le Brett I geboren. Sie hat 1320 William de Wasteneys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1332 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Colton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett è nata il circa 1298 nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlia di John le Brett I. Lei sposato William de Wasteneys nel 1320. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1332, nel Colton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brettは約1298年にイギリスイギリスエセックスでJohn le Brett Iの娘として生まれた。1320年にWilliam de Wasteneysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1332年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアコルトンで36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett(은)는 대략 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 John le Brett I의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1320년 William de Wasteneys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1332년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 콜턴에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett nasceu aproximadamente 1298, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filha de John le Brett I. Ela casou-se com William de Wasteneys em 1320. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1332, em Colton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Joanna le Brett родилась около 1298 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали John le Brett I. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Wasteneys в 1320 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1332 г. в местности колтон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 36 лет.
